name: Band Face Pull
resistance_profile: Ascending band tension with an external-rotation finish; hardest as the hands reach the face and the arms fully abduct and rotate — a peak-contraction bias that complements the more uniform cable version.
cues: Pull to the forehead, then rotate the knuckles back.
Keep the traps relaxed and the shoulders down.
execution_steps: Anchor a resistance band at roughly upper-chest to face height and grasp an end in each hand, stepping back to remove all slack.
Start with the arms extended toward the anchor, thumbs pointing back, and set a tall, braced torso.
Pull the band toward your forehead, flaring the elbows high and wide.
Externally rotate at the top so the knuckles finish beside or just above the ears.
Reverse slowly, letting the band draw the arms back to full extension while keeping the shoulders down.
good_when: You want rear-delt plus external-rotation work for shoulder health at home or in warm-ups.
No cable station is available and you want the face-pull pattern.
You want a high-rep, low-fatigue accessory to pair with pressing.
bad_when: You need maximal rear-delt loading for hypertrophy overload.
The anchor is too low or unstable to keep the elbows high.
common_errors: Low elbows that turn it into a straight row.
Skipping the external-rotation portion at the top.
Anchoring too low so the pull travels downward instead of to the face.
progressions: Add a 1-2 second external-rotation hold at the top.
Increase band tension by stepping further from the anchor.
regressions: Omit the external rotation and simply pull to the face.
